description: Integration: `getAvailableRecurringDueWork()` loads persisted client-cadence rows in a schema where `client_contract_lines` does not exist.
Integration: `getAvailableRecurringDueWork()` still loads contract-cadence rows in the same fully migrated schema.
Integration: materialization-gap detection for client cadence resolves obligations from the surviving structure without querying `client_contract_lines`.
UI: `AutomaticInvoices` render path loads successfully in a fully migrated environment with no dropped-table errors.
Integration: recurring preview for a client-cadence selector-input window succeeds without joining `client_contract_lines`.
Integration: recurring generate for a client-cadence selector-input window succeeds without joining `client_contract_lines`.
Integration: recurring preview/generate for contract cadence continues to work after client-line table removal cleanup.
Integration: service-period inspection resolves client-cadence obligation context without `client_contract_lines`.
Integration: client-cadence regeneration after billing schedule change loads obligations from surviving tables only.
DB-backed integration: deleting or reversing a recurring invoice repairs service-period linkage in a fully migrated schema with no client-line table.
Integration: invoice linkage backfills billed recurring rows using the canonical post-drop obligation identity rather than `client_contract_lines` lookups.
Integration: bucket period resolution for client cadence works in a schema with no `client_contract_lines` table.
Integration: contract wizard creates a client-owned contract and line successfully without inserting into dropped client-contract line tables.
Integration: contract wizard create flow does not attempt to insert `client_contract_services` in a fully migrated schema.
Integration: live contract line mutation/update flows remain functional after removing dropped-table writes or reads.
Integration: credit flow that previously looked up `client_contract_lines` still succeeds through the surviving client-owned structure.
Integration: billing/report overview read model returns live data without querying `client_contract_lines`.
Design validation: recurring client-cadence obligation identity is defined in one canonical way across due-work, regeneration, and linkage codepaths.
Static validation: no live recurring/invoicing action under `packages/billing/src/actions` queries `client_contract_lines`.
Static validation: no live billing service under `packages/billing/src/services` queries `client_contract_lines` for runtime logic.
Static validation: contract wizard/runtime create paths no longer insert into `client_contract_lines` or `client_contract_services`.
Static validation: server/package tests no longer assert `client_contract_lines` as expected live runtime behavior.
Documentation: new plan/runbook/docs language describes the post-drop client-owned structure without presenting `client_contract_lines` as a live dependency.
DB-backed sanity: a fully migrated schema with no dropped client-contract line tables can complete the `AutomaticInvoices` happy path from due-work selection to invoice creation.
Regression: contract-cadence recurring happy path remains functional after the post-drop cleanup.
Regression: client-owned contract loading still resolves line/runtime behavior through `client_contracts -> contracts -> contract_lines` after cleanup.

Integration: server-side contract-line service behaviors that remain live no longer query `client_contract_lines` for unassign/deactivate, analytics, in-use checks, overlap validation, or overview counts.
Documentation and hygiene: stale runbook SQL, cleanup-only fixtures, and tracked backup artifacts that reference dropped client-contract line tables are removed or explicitly marked historical.
